transcript.text: I keep putting the project off and feel like I'm falling behind everyone else, but I don't know how to start. It seems like my friends are moving forward while I'm stuck, and I just can't shake this anxious feeling. I wish I could figure out what to do next before I feel completely out of the loop.

You're feeling really stuck on this project, and it's like everyone around you is moving forward while you're still trying to figure out where to start. That can be a really overwhelming and anxious place to be. It sounds like the fear of not doing it perfectly is holding you back. Can you tell me more about what's going through your mind when you think about starting the project?

I'm not sure if I should say this, but I keep thinking I'm not good enough, and if I start the project, people will see how messed up I am. I also worry that this project is a sign of my career direction.

It takes a lot of courage to admit that you're worried about not being good enough and that people might see your flaws. I can sense how much anxiety you're carrying about this project, and it's understandable given the pressure you put on yourself to be perfect. Your fear that this project might be a sign of your career direction is also a heavy burden. Can you tell me more about what you think would happen if you didn't meet your own expectations with this project?

Honestly, if I don't meet my expectations, I think I'll feel like a failure and that my friends will see me as incompetent. That would just make me even more anxious and stuck, and I worry the project will be a sign that I'm not on the right career path.

You're really scared that if you don't meet your own expectations, you'll feel like a failure, and that fear is holding you back from starting the project. It's like you're carrying this weight of needing to be perfect, and it's making it hard for you to take that first step. Yeah, it makes sense that you'd worry about what others might think, but it sounds like the biggest critic you're facing is yourself. Can you think of a time when you've taken a risk and it's worked out, even if it wasn't perfect?

I guess the last time I took a risk was when I tried a new yoga class, but I still felt like I was on autopilot. It didn't feel like a big win, just a small step that made me anxious. And I'm not sure if this project will even show me the right direction.

You took a risk by trying that new yoga class, and even though it didn't feel like a big win, it was still a step forward. It's interesting that you mention feeling like you were on autopilot - it sounds like your anxiety was still present, even in a new and potentially exciting situation. That takes a lot of courage to acknowledge. How do you think you can apply that same courage to this project, even if it feels like a small step?
I think I might try breaking the project into tiny steps, but I'm still scared they'll see how messy it is. I keep feeling like I'm falling behind everyone else.

You're considering breaking the project into smaller steps, which is a great way to make it feel less overwhelming. It's understandable that you're still worried about what others might think, but it sounds like you're taking a step towards taking control of your project. What's the smallest step you could take right now to move forward, even if it feels a little messy?

I think the smallest step would be to jot down a quick outline, but the idea that it will look messy makes me anxious. I worry that if I show it, my friends will think I'm not pulling my weight, and that might mean I'm not on the right career path.

You're really hesitant to start that outline because you're worried it'll look messy and that your friends will judge you for it. It's like you're feeling this huge pressure to have everything figured out before you even begin, and that's a lot to carry. Yeah, it makes sense that you'd be anxious about what others might think, but it sounds like the biggest obstacle right now is your own fear of not being perfect. Can you think of a way to make that first step feel a little less daunting, maybe by reminding yourself that it's okay if it's not perfect?

I think I could start by writing a quick outline just for myself, not sharing it yet. But I'm still scared they'll think I'm not pulling my weight, and I worry it might show how stuck I am. I also wonder if this actually matters for my career. Maybe I can do it quietly, but the anxiety keeps me on the sidelines.

You're taking a really brave step by considering a private outline, just for yourself. That shows you're trying to find a way to move forward despite your fears. It's understandable that you're worried about what others might think, but it sounds like you're recognizing the importance of taking that first step, even if it's just for your eyes. What do you think would be the most comforting thing to remind yourself of as you start this outline, to help you feel more at ease with the process?
Honestly, I'm not sure what to remind myself of. Maybe just that it's okay to be messy, but the anxiety still feels heavy.

It's really tough to shake off that feeling of anxiety, especially when you're trying to remind yourself that it's okay to be messy. Yeah, it's like you're trying to give yourself permission to not be perfect, but that voice of self-criticism is still loud. I can see how you're trying to reassure yourself, and that takes a lot of courage. What if you tried to focus on the fact that you're taking small steps, like making that private outline, and that it's a process that doesn't have to be perfect from the start?

I’m still stuck because I keep thinking if I do this, my friends will see I’m stuck, especially after hearing about her pregnancy. It feels like I’m falling behind and I’m scared to show my work. Maybe it’s just me being anxious about everyone else moving forward.

You're feeling really stuck right now, and it's like the fear of being seen as imperfect is holding you back. Hearing about your friend's pregnancy has made you feel like you're falling behind, and that's a really tough feeling to navigate. It takes a lot of courage to admit when we're feeling stuck, and I want to acknowledge that it's okay to not have everything figured out. What's one thing you could do to take a small step forward, even if it feels scary or imperfect?

I could try jotting a tiny outline for myself, but I'm still scared they'll see how messy it is and think I'm stuck. Maybe just a quick note to my manager for clarification would help, but the fear of judgment keeps me on the sidelines.

You're still feeling really anxious about being judged, and that's making it hard for you to take even a small step like jotting down an outline. It's like that fear of being seen as imperfect is holding you back. Yeah, it's tough to shake off that feeling, but maybe taking a tiny step, like writing a quick note to your manager for clarification, could help you feel a little more in control. What do you think is the smallest thing you could do right now to move forward, even if it feels a little scary?

turn_traces.user_message: I keep putting the project off and feel like I'm falling behind everyone else, but I don't know how to start. It seems like my friends are moving forward while I'm stuck, and I just can't shake this anxious feeling. I wish I could figure out what to do next before I feel completely out of the loop.

My entry point will be to acknowledge the overwhelm and uncertainty you're experiencing, and then gently explore what might be underlying your hesitation to begin the project.

My entry point will be to acknowledge the user's fear of being seen as stuck and their anxiety about others moving forward, and to help them explore ways to reframe their thinking and build confidence in their abilities.
